chief_complaint: A 65-year-old male patient with a history of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) presented with a new diagnosis of right lower lobe lung cancer.
history: The patient underwent a sleeve right lower lobectomy (SRLL) at Akita University Hospital. He had squamous cell carcinoma pStage IB-IIB and was diagnosed with COPD. The patient's postoperative respiratory function exceeded predicted values.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 80 bpm, RR 20/min. Physical examination revealed a surgical scar on the right side of the chest.
labs: ECG: normal. Bloods: normal. Imaging: CT scan of the chest showed a mass in the right lower lobe. Other tests: bronchoscopy and biopsy confirmed squamous cell carcinoma.
correct_diagnosis: Squamous cell carcinoma of the right lower lobe
differentials: Squamous cell carcinoma of the right lower lobe
Adenocarcinoma of the right lower lobe
Small cell carcinoma of the right lower lobe
Bronchial carcinoid tumor of the right lower lobe
Metastatic lung cancer from an unknown primary site
correct_ranking: Squamous cell carcinoma of the right lower lobe
Adenocarcinoma of the right lower lobe
Small cell carcinoma of the right lower lobe
Bronchial carcinoid tumor of the right lower lobe
Metastatic lung cancer from an unknown primary site
